text: hi
hi
hi

Generate a file named AGENTS.md that serves as a contributor guide for this repository.
Your goal is to produce a clear, concise, and well-structured document with descriptive headings and actionable explanations for each section.
Follow the outline below, but adapt as needed — add sections if relevant, and omit those that do not apply to this project.

Document Requirements

- Title the document "Repository Guidelines".
- Use Markdown headings (#, ##, etc.) for structure.
- Keep the document concise. 200-400 words is optimal.
- Keep explanations short, direct, and specific to this repository.
- Provide examples where helpful (commands, directory paths, naming patterns).
- Maintain a professional, instructional tone.

Recommended Sections

Project Structure & Module Organization

- Outline the project structure, including where the source code, tests, and assets are located.

Build, Test, and Development Commands

- List key commands for building, testing, and running locally (e.g., npm test, make build).
- Briefly explain what each command does.

Coding Style & Naming Conventions

- Specify indentation rules, language-specific style preferences, and naming patterns.
- Include any formatting or linting tools used.

Testing Guidelines

- Identify testing frameworks and coverage requirements.
- State test naming conventions and how to run tests.

Commit & Pull Request Guidelines

- Summarize commit message conventions found in the project’s Git history.
- Outline pull request requirements (descriptions, linked issues, screenshots, etc.).

(Optional) Add other sections if relevant, such as Security & Configuration Tips, Architecture Overview, or Agent-Specific Instructions.

$comment InflowRightContent/index

将以下内容生成为skill: name: CodeCommentArchitectSkill
version: 1.0

description: >
  Generate professional engineering-level code comments with architecture context.

scope:
  - props destructuring
  - custom hooks
  - custom functions
  - internal components
  - business utilities

input:
  code: string
  filePath: string
  projectContext: optional string
  relatedFiles: optional array

output:
  format: markdown
  language: English

workflow:
  - analyze_context
  - identify_core_units
  - trace_call_chain
  - evaluate_design
  - generate_comments
  - build_architecture_graph

comment_rules:

  props:
    - business meaning
    - dependency source
    - validation responsibility

  hooks:
    - lifecycle role
    - data source
    - side effects
    - return semantics

  functions:
    - purpose
    - input constraints
    - output guarantees
    - error handling

  components:
    - UI responsibility
    - rendering boundary
    - reuse scope
    - performance concerns

output_template:

  sections:
    - Overview
    - Responsibilities
    - Call Chain
    - Advantages
    - Code Comments
    - Architecture Diagram
使用skill规范，提示词规范帮我生成skill，用于 注释(comment)代码。内容为: 需要注释的代码类型范围(props解构的变量，自定义hooks及其返回值，自定义函数及其返回值，项目内自定义组件等到)，注释的内容方向:例如，作用，调用链，职责范围，代码优点等等，并在了解其在项目中的功能后，在末尾生成 关键调用 或 关键引用组件结构图(architecture_graph)与注释。
comment-code 放全局 中文 风格偏好混合 纯文本树状 包含所有项目内的自定义组件并在图中简短标明其位置,并添加前面要求的注释

修改skill/comment code/skill.md # Component Tree

App
├── Layout               # Page layout container
│   ├── Header           # Top navigation bar
│   │   ├── Logo         # Brand logo
│   │   └── NavMenu      # Main navigation
│   ├── Main
│   │   ├── Sidebar      # Left sidebar
│   │   └── Content      # Main content area
│   │       ├── ArticleList
│   │       └── Pagination
│   └── Footer           # Footer area
使用上述 architecture_graph 结构,并在注释末尾增加文件路径,该tree表示当前组件引用的项目内自定义组件调用结构,层级不超过3层,每个组件都要有 #注释注明其 职责范围. 在树末尾增加对当前组件或其调用组件的评价
不要将行内注释添加在代码末尾,这不符合代码规范

下面的.zshrc配置是什么作用 export CNB_TOKEN="" # your token

alias mrm="git push origin \$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD):mr/master/\$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) && git cnb pull create -H mr/master/\$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) -B master"

alias mrt="git push origin \$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD):mr/test/\$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) && git cnb pull create -H mr/test/\$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) -B test"

# 发起发布 预发布环境的ci 流程
alias mrp="git push origin \$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD):mr/prerelease/\$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) && git cnb pull create -H mr/prerelease/\$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD) -B prerelease"

macos 安装vscode如何安装

下面的vscode插件什么作用:Table of Contents
Features
Usage
Commands
Configurations
Roadmap
License
Features
🚀 One-click setup - Add project links directly in VS Code settings
⚡ Instant access - Open links with just a few keystrokes via Command Palette
🔧 Custom links - Define your own link resources with custom titles and URLs
🔍 Smart detection - Auto-discovers links from GitHub, CODING, and CNB repositories
🎯 Dynamic templates - Use {{repo.url}}, {{git.branch}} variables for context-aware links (see all variables)
📊 Tree view - Browse organized links with search and filtering in sidebar
📈 Status bar - Quick link count and access right from the status bar
👥 Team sharing - Share links via remote CSV files for team collaboration
Usage
Add your project links to .vscode/settings.json:
{
  "links.resources": [
    {
      "url": "https://github.com/yunsii/vscode-links",
      "title": "GitHub Repository"
    }
    // Add more links as needed
  ]
}

Open the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P or Cmd+Shift+P on macOS).

Type Links: Open... and select it.

Choose the link you want to open in your default browser 🎉.

Commands
Command	Title
links.openPicker	Links: Open Picker
links.refresh	Links: Refresh
links.enterSearch	Links: Enter Search Mode
links.exitSearch	Links: Exit Search Mode
links.openUrl	Links: Open Link
links.copyUrl	Links: Copy Link URL
Configurations
Key	Description	Type	Default
links.resources	Local resources to open	array	``
links.remoteResources	Remote resources to open: set to an object with url and project. Leave unset (null) to disable.	undefined	``
links.customIcons	Custom icons for different link types	object	{ "local": "folder", "detected": "eye", "remote-project": "repo", "remote-shared": "share" }
Remote Resources Example
To use remote CSV resources, add this to your .vscode/settings.json:

{
  "links.remoteResources": {
    "url": "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vRlzHErycXFRcNf2u9kB3SndusKyGaAEmoh2gueEKEtkTaibfMKKgOonlJhgBArqKrKJJlXthTo7LFN/pub?gid=0&single=true&output=csv",
    "project": "my-project"
  }
}

The CSV file should have columns: project (required), url (required), title (required), description (optional).

How it works:

Links with project matching your configured project name appear as project-specific links
Links with project: "#shared-links" appear as shared team links
Other links are filtered out automatically
Roadmap
Localization support for multiple languages
License
MIT License © 2025 Yuns

OverflowTooltipText 是不是太复杂了 ⚠️ 3. 只在内容溢出时才 show Tooltip

Ant Design 官方本身没有内置检测文本是否真正溢出的功能（Tooltip 会始终显示）——想要 “只有真正溢出才显示 Tooltip” 的智能行为，你可以：

自行测量元素是否溢出（scrollWidth > clientWidth）

根据检测结果有条件地渲染 Tooltip
